name: task-driver
description: Phase 1 of the six-phase development lifecycle. Two entry modes: Pickup (existing flow — read ROADMAP, score-and-rank shortlist, plan-mode design, implement with TODO tracking, update all project docs) and Plan-and-File (new in v1.2 — research → draft plan → EnterPlanMode → on ExitPlanMode approval, save_issue to Linear with status Todo OR ROADMAP-fallback row + .thoughts/plans/<id>.md, then STOP — fresh implementer session picks up). Plan-and-File preserves the implementer/reviewer separation principle: same session that designs a plan should not also implement against it without a clean session boundary. Phase Awareness header in SKILL.md (predecessor: user; successor: implementer in Phase 2). v1.4.0: add `task-writing` skill — mirror of the task-writing include (5-question pre-creation gate, task-as-prompt vs over-specification, tasks.toml field set) so it's skill-reachable on Opus 4.8 without an eager @-import.
